items: The frontend (or browser) sends a message to your backend. This message includes: the URL they want (/api/hello), the method (GET, POST, etc.), and sometimes extra data.
Your Flask code receives the request, runs your function, and prepares a response.
Your backend sends back data (usually JSON) along with a status code. 200 means "everything went fine." 404 means "not found." 500 means "something went wrong on the server."
